lessons.title: Literal vs. Inferential Meaning
objectives: Distinguish between what is explicitly stated and what is implied
Identify main ideas and supporting details
concepts: Literal meaning: The surface level meaning (factual).
Inferential meaning: Reading between the lines (deducing based on evidence).

experiment.description: Evidence Hunter: Read a short passage and highlight the specific sentences that support a given inference.

lessons.title: Figurative Language
objectives: Identify and explain similes, metaphors, and personification
Analyze the effect of figurative language on the reader
concepts: Personification: Giving human traits to non-human things.

experiment.description: Figure of Speech Match: Drag literary devices to their corresponding examples in a series of poem snippets.

lessons.title: Identifying Main Points
objectives: Extract key information from a passage
concepts: A summary must be objective and concise.
Avoid examples and repetitions in a summary.

experiment.description: Word Count Squeeze: Start with a 100-word paragraph and click on words/sentences to 'delete' them, aiming to reach a 30-word summary that still retains the core meaning.

lessons.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
objectives: Ensure subjects and verbs agree in number
Handle collective nouns and indefinite pronouns

experiment.description: Agreement Lab: A series of sentences appear with blank verbs. Select the correct verb form from a dropdown to make the sentence grammatically correct.

lessons.title: Punctuation Mastery
objectives: Use commas, colons, and semi-colons correctly
concepts: Colon: introduces a list or explanation.

experiment.description: Punctuation Placement: Drag punctuation marks ( , ; : . ) into an unpunctuated paragraph to restore its meaning.

lessons.title: Synonyms and Antonyms
objectives: Identify words with similar or opposite meanings
Use context clues to determine word meaning
concepts: Synonyms: Words with similar meanings (e.g., happy/joyful).
Antonyms: Words with opposite meanings (e.g., hot/cold).

experiment.description: Context Clue Challenge: Replace a highlighted word in a sentence with its best synonym from a list, ensuring the sentence's meaning remains intact.

lessons.title: Narrative Plot Structure
objectives: Understand the 5 stages of plot: Exposition, Rising Action, Climax, Falling Action, Resolution
concepts: Every story needs a conflict and a resolution.

experiment.description: Story Arc Builder: Drag shuffled plot events onto a 'Story Mountain' diagram to arrange them in the correct narrative sequence.

lessons.title: Structuring an Argument
concepts: Introduction, Body Paragraphs (PEEL: Point, Evidence, Explanation, Link), Conclusion.

experiment.description: Argument Organizer: Drag and drop 'Intro', 'Body Paragraphs', and 'Conclusion' blocks into the correct order to form a coherent essay structure.
